exercises.title: Syndrome Table Builder
exercises.description: Build and use a syndrome lookup table for decoding.
tasks: Compute all possible syndromes from H matrix
Map each syndrome to minimum-weight error pattern
Handle cases where multiple patterns have same weight
Decode received words using the table
Test on Hamming [7,4,3] code
hints: Only add multi-bit errors if syndrome not already mapped

exercises.title: Maximum Likelihood Decoder
exercises.description: Implement brute-force ML decoding with probability analysis.
tasks: Generate all 2^k codewords
Find nearest codeword to received word
Calculate probability of each codeword given BSC
Detect ties (multiple equally-likely codewords)
Compare results with syndrome decoder
hints: Lower distance = higher probability when p < 0.5
ML and syndrome should agree for correctable errors

exercises.title: Coset Structure Analyzer
exercises.description: Analyze the coset structure of a linear code.
tasks: Group all 2^n words by syndrome (coset)
Find coset leader for each coset
Verify each coset has 2^k elements
Calculate covering radius
Visualize coset weight distribution
hints: Code is perfect iff covering radius = t

exercises.title: Decoder Performance Comparator
exercises.description: Compare decoding methods on various codes and error rates.
tasks: Implement both ML and syndrome decoders
Simulate BSC with various error rates
Measure word error rate after decoding
Compare computational time
Identify break-even points
hints: Use many random trials for accurate statistics
ML is slower but should give same WER
